all_dirs.description: If true, all directories under the specified paths are mountable.

block_size.description: The block size returned by the NFS STATFS procedure.

can_set_time.description: If true, the client may  set file  times using the NFS SETATTR request.  This  option is advisory and the server always behaves as if it is true.

clients.description: Clients that have access to the export.

commit_asynchronous.description: If true, allows NFS  commit  requests to  execute asynchronously.

conflicting_paths.description: Paths that conflict with another export.

description.description: A human readable description of the export.

directory_transfer_size.description: The preferred size for directory read operations.  This option is advisory.

encoding.description: The character encoding of clients connecting to the export.

map_all.description: The user and groups that non-root clients are mapped to.

map_full.description: If true, user mappings queries the OneFS user database.  If false, only local authentication is queried.

map_lookup_uid.description: If true, incoming UIDs are mapped to users in the OneFS user database.  If false, incoming UIDs are applied directly to file operations.

map_retry.description: Determines whether lookups for users specified in map_all or map_root are retried if the look fails.

map_root.description: The user and groups that root clients are mapped to.

max_file_size.description: The maximum file size in the export.

paths.description: The paths under /ifs that are exported.

read_only.description: If true, the export is read-only.

read_only_clients.description: Clients that have read only access to the export.

read_transfer_max_size.description: The maximum buffer size that clients should use on NFS read requests.  This option is advisory.

read_transfer_multiple.description: The preferred multiple size for NFS read requests.  This option is advisory.

read_transfer_size.description: The optimal size for NFS read requests.  This option is advisory.

read_write_clients.description: Clients that have read and write access to the export, even if the export is read-only.

readdirplus.description: If true, readdirplus requests are enabled.

readdirplus_prefetch.description: Sets the number of directory entries that will be prefetched when a readdirplus request is processed.

return_32bit_file_ids.description: Limits the size of file identifiers returned by NFSv3+ to 32-bit values (may require remount).

root_clients.description: Clients that have root access to the export.

security_flavors.description: The authentication flavors that are supported for this export.

setattr_asynchronous.description: If true, allows setattr operations to execute asynchronously.

snapshot.description: Use this snapshot for all mounts.

symlinks.description: If true, paths reachable by symlinks are exported.

time_delta.description: The resolution of all time values that are returned to clients.

write_datasync_action.description: The action to be taken when an NFSv3+ datasync write is requested.

write_datasync_reply.description: The stability disposition returned when an NFSv3+ datasync write is processed.

write_filesync_action.description: The action to be taken when an NFSv3+ filesync write is requested.

write_filesync_reply.description: The stability disposition returned when an NFSv3+ filesync write is processed.

write_transfer_max_size.description: The maximum buffer size that clients should use on NFS write requests.  This option is advisory.

write_transfer_multiple.description: The preferred multiple size for NFS write requests.  This option is advisory.

write_transfer_size.description: The optimal size for NFS read requests.  This option is advisory.

write_unstable_action.description: The action to be taken when an NFSv3+ unstable write is requested.

write_unstable_reply.description: The stability disposition returned when an NFSv3+ unstable write is processed.
